text:
Once upon a time, deep within the tranquil woods lived a curious fox named Felix. Every dawn, he'd trot over to the village’s old oak tree, nestled amid whispering bushes, counting the bright autumn leaves that glittered like jewels under the morning sun.

Felix had heard tales of a legendary golden feather fallen from the great eagle constellation. It was said that whoever discovered this feather could hear the wise thoughts of the birds themselves.

One peaceful morning, after counting his hundredteenth feather, Felix decided it was time for adventure. He embarked on his quest towards the east, hoping to reach the great constellation. He encountered many creatures along the way – a talking owl that danced every breeze, a mischievous squirrel that chuckled like a brook, and even a wise old owl perched atop a great oak that listened intently to every nocturnal chirp.

Days turned into nights, and nights into days, with Felix growing colder and his fur turning an icy grey. Just as he was
